title: [HIGH] conformance: SpecialExpectedOutcome::ErrorKind comparison uses format!('{:?}', kind) string match — breaks when Debug repr changes (enum variants grow fields) AND conflates fixture-schema DomainError with genuine domain violations
description:
Review of crates/fsci-conformance/src/lib.rs:10997-11006 on 2026-04-22:

    (SpecialExpectedOutcome::ErrorKind { error }, Err(actual)) => {
        let observed_kind = format!('{:?}', actual.kind);
        let pass = observed_kind == *error;
        ...
    }

Problems:

1. Debug-format coupling. Rust's Debug repr is NOT a stable string contract. If FsciSpecialErrorKind::DomainError grows to DomainError { detail: String }, the Debug output changes from 'DomainError' to 'DomainError { detail: "..." }'. All 10 adversarial cases (case.category == 'adversarial') using expected.kind='error_kind' + expected.error='DomainError' silently flip pass -> fail without any actual behavior change.

2. Inconsistency with Class comparator. At 10987-10989 the ValueClass comparison uses typed enum equality (observed_class == *class). The ErrorKind comparator should mirror this pattern: compare typed enums, not Debug strings. The Class path is the right pattern; the ErrorKind path regresses to the string-compare anti-pattern documented in gnun.

3. Masks fixture-schema bugs. Per frankenscipy-04jy, special_invalid_fixture_error mints FsciSpecialErrorKind::DomainError for ANY argument-count mismatch (191 sites). So a fixture like:
       case_id: 'adv_gamma_pole_hardened_error'
       function: 'gamma'
       args: [-2.0, 3.0, 1.0]   # typo: should be [-2.0]
       category: 'adversarial'
       expected: {kind: 'error_kind', error: 'DomainError'}
   would:
   - enter execute_special_case at 10509 (actually gamma takes 1 arg, not covered there; use a 1-arg fn)
   - actual handler sees wrong arity, returns special_invalid_fixture_error (DomainError kind)
   - compare_special_case_differential sees observed_kind='DomainError'
   - matches expected='DomainError' -> PASS
   - claim 'verified gamma fails closed on pole' even though we hit an arity check before touching gamma
   The test is lying about what it verified.

Fix:
1. Introduce a typed comparison path. Parse expected.error into FsciSpecialErrorKind (perhaps via FromStr or a lookup table) and compare enum vs enum:
       let expected_kind: FsciSpecialErrorKind = error.parse()
           .map_err(|_| (false, format!('unknown error kind: {error}'), None, None))?;
       let pass = actual.kind == expected_kind;
2. Separate fixture-schema errors from domain errors (ties to 04jy) -- a fixture-schema error should make the case UNJUDGEABLE (per ihex), not match a DomainError expectation.
3. Mirror the Class comparator pattern at 10987.

Related: gnun (error-string equality anti-pattern), 04jy (DomainError overloaded for fixture-schema), ihex (both-errored = pass amplification), tfd7 (metamorphic self-consistency is strength-4 without strength-1 cross-check).

title: fsci-conformance: add diff_ndimage_gaussian_laplace_gradient_magnitude — gaussian_laplace + gaussian_gradient_magnitude parity

Divergence is structural, not a precision issue. Filed defect bead frankenscipy-1vmzg.

title: fsci-stats: pearsonr_alternative + spearmanr_alternative return 0 for all alternatives when |r|=1
description: P3: fsci's pearsonr_alternative and spearmanr_alternative (crates/fsci-stats/src/lib.rs ~14545 and ~14622) returned pvalue = 0.0 for ALL alternatives when |r| ≥ 1.0. For perfect monotone fixtures (r = ±1) scipy returns pvalue ≈ 1 for the contradicting tail and 0 for the agreeing tail; fsci was returning 0 for both. Fix: dispatch on alternative when r >= 1.0 and r <= -1.0 — perfect positive sets less→1 / greater→0; perfect negative sets less→0 / greater→1. Surfaced by diff_stats_ansari_spearmanr_alts.rs (and would also surface for pearsonr_alternative). Fixed inline.

title: [code-review] periodogram_simple uses fft on complex-promoted real input — should use rfft
description:
fsci-fft's periodogram_simple at crates/fsci-fft/src/helpers.rs:231 takes a real signal x: &[f64], allocates a complex_input: Vec<(f64, f64)> twice the size, runs the full complex fft (which produces N complex outputs), then keeps only the first N/2+1 bins. This is two unnecessary costs on a real-input hot path:

  1. Allocation: complex_input doubles the input footprint (N (f64, f64) pairs vs N f64).
  2. Compute: fft computes N complex bins, but symmetry says only N/2+1 are independent for real input.

scipy.signal.periodogram uses rfft internally. fsci-fft already has rfft (line 444 of transforms.rs); switching periodogram_simple to call rfft directly halves the FFT work and removes the intermediate Vec.

P1 because periodogram is a common hot path in spectral analysis. Acceptance: periodogram_simple stops allocating the complex_input Vec; calls rfft(x) directly; produces identical (within f64 precision) output to the current implementation; existing tests still pass.

title: [b6z3m sub] fsci-special jn_zeros first k zeros of J_n
description: Slice of frankenscipy-b6z3m. Add scipy.special.jn_zeros(n, k) returning first k positive zeros of Bessel J_n. Uses McMahon's asymptotic expansion j_{n,k} ≈ (k + n/2 - 1/4)π · (1 - (4n²-1)/(8μ²) + ...) where μ = (4k + 2n - 1)π/4 as initial guess, then refines via bisection in a 0.5-radius bracket (same robustness pattern as ai_zeros). Add metamorphic relations: J_n(zero) ≈ 0, zeros are positive and monotonically increasing, first zero of J_0 ≈ 2.4048.

title: fsci-conformance: scipy diff for distribution mode
description: Add diff_stats_mode.rs cross-checking distribution mode formulas across fsci continuous distributions vs the analytic mode formula computed in subprocess (scipy doesn't expose a generic .mode but the analytic mode is well-known per distribution). Tolerances: 1e-13 abs (closed-form).
close_reason: diff_stats_mode shipped: 13 distributions vs analytic mode oracle in subprocess. Tol 1e-13 abs (closed-form). scipy doesn't expose a generic .mode() so the oracle uses well-known per-distribution analytic forms (Weibull mode = σ·((c-1)/c)^(1/c), Lognormal mode = scale·exp(-s²), Beta mode = (a-1)/(a+b-2), etc.). Coverage orthogonal to cdf/pdf/ppf/entropy/moments paths.

title: [MEDIUM] fsci-linalg: solve_circulant silently zeros out near-singular FFT-space modes (|λ|²<1e-30) instead of raising — produces wrong 'solution' that doesn't satisfy Cx=b for singular/ill-conditioned circulants; scipy.linalg.solve_circulant raises LinAlgError by default via singular='raise'; no input finiteness check either
description:
Review of crates/fsci-linalg/src/lib.rs on 2026-04-23.

solve_circulant (line 4308-4348) silently zeros out near-singular frequency
components rather than raising LinAlgError, producing wrong answers for
singular or ill-conditioned circulant systems.

Problematic code (line 4330-4342):

    let fft_x: Vec<(f64, f64)> = fft_b
        .iter()
        .zip(fft_c.iter())
        .map(|(&(br, bi), &(cr, ci))| {
            let denom = cr * cr + ci * ci;
            if denom < 1e-30 {
                (0.0, 0.0)                                  // <-- silent singularity mask
            } else {
                ((br * cr + bi * ci) / denom, (bi * cr - br * ci) / denom)
            }
        })
        .collect();

Analysis:

A circulant matrix C has FFT-space diagonalization C = F^* diag(λ) F, where
λ_k = FFT(c)[k]. Solving Cx = b becomes x̂_k = b̂_k / λ_k.

When λ_k ≈ 0, the system is singular (or nearly so). The code silently sets
x̂_k = 0 in that case, producing a 'solution' that fails to satisfy Cx = b
when b̂_k ≠ 0. This is a classic 'return wrong answer silently on singular
input' bug.

Scipy comparison:
- scipy.linalg.solve_circulant has two extra parameters:
    tol: float, default 1e-16 * max(|eigenvalues|)
    singular: 'raise' (default) or 'lstsq'
  With singular='raise', raises scipy.linalg.LinAlgError when any |λ_k|
  falls below tol.
  With singular='lstsq', computes least-squares solution using 0 for the
  null-space modes.
- Our implementation is silently a 'lstsq'-mode solver with tol fixed at
  sqrt(1e-30) ≈ 3e-16 of the eigenvalue magnitude squared. No 'raise' mode,
  no user-configurable tolerance.

Impact:

1. Users migrating from scipy see different behavior: scipy raises on
   singular input (default); our impl silently returns a wrong solution.

2. The 'wrong' solution satisfies C * x ≈ P_range(C) * b (projection of b
   onto range of C), not Cx = b. For users expecting exact solution, this
   is a correctness bug.

3. Detection requires checking ||Cx - b|| afterward, which users shouldn't
   have to do for a solve function that's advertised as a 'solver.'

4. NaN fall-open: if cr or ci is NaN (which shouldn't happen given earlier
   NaN validation at the solve entry points, but solve_circulant doesn't
   have any own validation), denom = NaN, NaN < 1e-30 is false, takes
   else branch, divides by NaN, output NaN silently. No input validation
   on the circulant vector c.

Additional concerns in solve_circulant:

a. No validation that c is finite. Line 4308 is:
       pub fn solve_circulant(c: &[f64], b: &[f64]) -> Result<Vec<f64>, LinalgError>
   No mode parameter. No check_finite parameter. No Hardened-mode path.
   Other solve functions (solve, solve_triangular, solve_banded) take
   SolveOptions with mode and check_finite. Inconsistent API.

b. Divergence threshold 1e-30 is on denom = |λ|² not |λ|. So the cutoff
   is at |λ| ≈ 1e-15, close to machine epsilon for 1.0-scale matrices.
   For small-scale matrices this is far too permissive; for large-scale
   it's too strict. Scipy scales tol by max |eigenvalue|.

Fix:

1. Add a `tol` parameter (or use a default based on max|eigenvalue|):
       let max_lambda_sq = fft_c.iter().map(|&(cr, ci)| cr*cr + ci*ci).fold(0.0, f64::max);
       let tol = 1e-28 * max_lambda_sq;  // or parameterized

2. Detect singularity and raise:
       if denom < tol {
           if br*br + bi*bi > tol * tol * max_b_sq {
               return Err(LinalgError::SingularMatrix);
           }
           (0.0, 0.0)  // only if b̂_k is also zero
       }

3. Add SolveOptions-based variant for scipy-parity mode control:
       pub fn solve_circulant_with_options(..., options: SolveOptions) -> ...

4. Validate c and b for finiteness at function entry.

Regression tests:
    let c = vec![1.0, -2.0, 1.0];  // circulant with eigenvalue 0 (λ[1] = 1 - 2*e^{2πi/3} + e^{4πi/3} = 0 for certain c)
    let b = vec![1.0, 0.0, 0.0];   // b̂ not in range of C
    let x = solve_circulant(&c, &b);
    assert!(x.is_err());  // currently returns Ok with wrong answer

Related: l15l (procrustes silent divergence via Newton-Schulz), wofb
(fsci-stats sf precision loss), n240 (cluster metrics silent 0.0),
42z1 (mmread DoS), 1dpl (RBF silent NaN). Pattern: functions that should
signal computational failure but instead return degraded-quality output.
close_reason: Fail-closed on singular solve_circulant (commit 818d02d).

title: drss follow-up: nuttall coefficient fix + 9 multi-arg windows
description:
drss closed with 3 1-arg windows (parzen/bohman/lanczos) in a127748. Remaining work:
1. Fix fsci_signal::nuttall_window coefficients to match scipy's minimum-4-term Blackman-Harris convention.
2. Extend SignalCase args schema to accept param vector for multi-arg windows.
3. Add 9 more windows: tukey (alpha), gaussian (std, sym), exponential (center, tau, sym), kaiser_bessel_derived (beta, sym), general_cosine (coeffs, sym), general_hamming (alpha), chebwin (at), taylor (nbar, sll, norm, sym), dpss (NW, Kmax).

Acceptance: each new window gets at least 1 fixture case bit-exact against scipy.signal.windows at rtol=1e-9.
notes: Fixed via eeb8a54. dpss dispatch wired with (n, nw, sym) -> Approximate normalization, kmax=None single taper. Two fixture cases at rtol=1e-9. All 12 multi-arg windows from acceptance now have fixtures: parzen, bohman, lanczos, nuttall, tukey, gaussian, exponential, kaiser_bessel_derived, general_cosine, general_hamming, chebwin, taylor, dpss.

title: Extend HalfspaceIntersection beyond the current 2D-only surface
description:
Evidence:
- [crates/fsci-spatial/src/lib.rs](/data/projects/frankenscipy/crates/fsci-spatial/src/lib.rs:1440) still rejects any `HalfspaceIntersection::from_nd` call whose interior point is not 2D.
- The existing implementation and conformance work closed the initial 2D parity task, but SciPy's `scipy.spatial.HalfspaceIntersection` is an N-dimensional API.

Scope:
- Generalize `HalfspaceIntersection` input validation, construction, and result metadata beyond 2D.
- Preserve the existing 2D behavior and error semantics while adding explicit higher-dimensional support or a clearly documented V1 cut line.
- Add conformance fixtures for at least one 3D bounded halfspace system and one higher-dimensional invalid-input case.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` / spatial docs to reflect the actual supported dimensionality.

Suggested files:
- `crates/fsci-spatial/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fsci-conformance/tests/e2e_spatial.rs`
close_reason: Already implemented on HEAD

title: fsci-stats: pin ExponPow(b=1) ≡ Gompertz(c=1) metamorphic identity
description: Pin the textbook metamorphic identity ExponPow(b=1) ≡ Gompertz(c=1). Both pdf forms reduce to exp(1+x−exp(x)) for x ≥ 0. Independent verification across two distinct code paths: ExponPow uses logpdf = 1 + ln(b) + (b-1)·ln(x) + xb − exp(xb) summed and exped; Gompertz uses self.c·x.exp()·(-self.c·(x.exp()-1)).exp(). Pin pdf/cdf/sf agreement at multiple x.
close_reason: Pinned the textbook metamorphic identity ExponPow(b=1) ≡ Gompertz(c=1). Both pdf forms reduce to exp(1 + x − exp(x)) for x ≥ 0. Test exponpow_b1_is_gompertz_c1 sweeps 7 x-values comparing pdf/cdf/sf at tol 1e-12 (sf at 1e-9 for tail saturation). Independent verification across two distinct code paths: ExponPow uses logpdf = 1 + ln(b) + (b−1)·ln(x) + xb − exp(xb); Gompertz uses c·exp(x)·exp(-c·(exp(x)−1)). The algebraic equivalence holds only if both implementations are correct. fsci-stats --lib: 1060 → 1061.

title: fsci-stats: golden-artifact fixture for powerlognorm
description: PowerLognorm has anchor + scipy diff (3753cd6) + fuzz (3a95e66, 4 real defect fixes) + metamorphic (972ee8c); complete coverage with frozen golden fixture so drift is caught even on rch workers without scipy. Generate FSCI-P2C-007_powerlognorm_golden.json with 4 c-values × 4 s-values × 6 x-values for pdf/cdf/sf and 4 c × 4 s × 5 q-values for ppf at scipy 1.17.1. Add crates/fsci-conformance/tests/golden_stats_powerlognorm.rs that loads it offline and pins at realistic tolerances mirroring the conformance harness (1e-7 abs for pdf/cdf/sf to absorb deep-tail Φ amplification, 5e-6 abs for ppf).
close_reason: Added crates/fsci-conformance/fixtures/FSCI-P2C-007_powerlognorm_golden.json (frozen scipy 1.17.1: 4 c-values × 4 s-values × 6 x-values = 96 points × 3 families = 288 + 4 c × 4 s × 5 q-values = 80 ppf rows) and crates/fsci-conformance/tests/golden_stats_powerlognorm.rs that loads and pins offline. Tolerances mirror diff_stats_powerlognorm.rs: 1e-7 abs for pdf/cdf/sf (powf(Phi(-z), c-1) amplifies Phi precision in deep tails) and 5e-6 abs for ppf (exp(s*Phi^-1) amplifies BSM ~1e-9 floor). All cases pass with max diffs: pdf=1.3e-8, cdf=7e-9, sf=7e-9, ppf=1.8e-6 — well within budget. Catches future drift offline. Completes PowerLognorm 5-tier coverage (port + diff + fuzz + metamorphic + golden) — fifth distribution to reach this tier in this session (after KsTwoBign, Kappa3, Burr3, WeibullMax).

title: fsci-stats: pin HalfLogistic ≡ folded Logistic identity
description: Pin the textbook metamorphic identity HalfLogistic ≡ |Logistic(0, 1)|: HalfLogistic.pdf(x) = 2·Logistic(0,1).pdf(x); HalfLogistic.cdf(x) = 2·Logistic(0,1).cdf(x) - 1; HalfLogistic.sf(x) = 2·Logistic(0,1).sf(x). Independent verification across two distinct code paths — Logistic uses exp/(1+exp)² while HalfLogistic uses 2·exp(-x)/(1+exp(-x))² (algebraically equivalent for x ≥ 0).
close_reason: Pinned the textbook metamorphic identity HalfLogistic ≡ |Logistic(0, 1)| in crates/fsci-stats/src/lib.rs. For x ≥ 0: HalfLogistic.pdf(x) = 2·Logistic.pdf(x); HalfLogistic.cdf(x) = 2·Logistic.cdf(x)−1; HalfLogistic.sf(x) = 2·Logistic.sf(x). Test halflogistic_is_folded_logistic sweeps 7 x-values comparing all three relations at tol 1e-12. Independent verification across two distinct code paths — Logistic uses exp/(1+exp)² with translation/scale handling; HalfLogistic uses 2·exp(-x)/(1+exp(-x))² directly. The algebraic equivalence at x ≥ 0 holds only if both implementations are correct. fsci-stats --lib: 1049 → 1050.

title: fsci_spatial::jaccard semantics drift on real-valued inputs vs scipy ≥1.15
description: scipy 1.15+ jaccard coerces non-0/1 numeric input to Boolean before computation (per scipy 1.15 changelog). fsci_spatial::jaccard treats real-valued inputs as 'count unequal positions / count nonzero positions', which diverges from scipy by exactly 1.0 in the small_2d/larger_3d/one_d_long fixtures. Surfaced via diff_spatial_pdist_cdist (frankenscipy-ixht7). Suggested fix: in fsci's jaccard, convert input to bool (x != 0.0) before computing the contingency counts, matching scipy semantics. Note: the existing diff_spatial_boolean_distances already covers boolean-input jaccard cleanly — this is specifically about numeric input.

title: fsci-stats: metamorphic — Kappa3(a=1) ≡ Lomax(c=1)
description: Kappa3(a=1) is algebraically identical to Lomax(c=1): both have pdf = (1+x)^(-2) and cdf = 1 - 1/(1+x) = x/(1+x). Pin this in fsci-stats lib as kappa3_a1_matches_lomax_c1 across 8 x-values [0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 50, 100], asserting max abs diff < 1e-13 for both pdf and cdf. Catches future drift in either Kappa3 or Lomax that would break this identity (and they SHOULD remain identical at this point in parameter space).
close_reason: Added kappa3_a1_matches_lomax_c1 metamorphic test in fsci-stats lib. Drives 8 x-values [0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 50, 100] and asserts max abs diff < 1e-13 for both pdf and cdf between Kappa3(a=1) and Lomax(c=1). The identity is exact: both reduce to pdf=(1+x)^(-2), cdf=x/(1+x). Catches future drift in either family that would break this structural equivalence. fsci-stats --lib: 1103 -> 1104.

title: [HIGH] conformance CI: G3 runs golden_journeys+schema_validation (not differential); G5 runs only e2e_linalg (1 of 16 e2e); G6 runs only perf_linalg (1 of 8 perf); G3 and G7 duplicate schema_validation
description:
Review of crates/fsci-conformance/src/ci_gates.rs:60-82 on 2026-04-22:

Gate command audit:
  G1Lint       -> cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy (OK)
  G2UnitTests  -> cargo test --workspace (broad, OK)
  G3Conformance-> golden_journeys + schema_validation       <-- but named 'Differential Conformance'
  G4Adversarial-> smoke                                     <-- smoke test != adversarial
  G5E2e        -> e2e_linalg                                <-- 1 of 16 e2e_*.rs files
  G6Performance-> perf_linalg                               <-- 1 of 8 perf_*.rs files
  G7Schema     -> schema_validation                         <-- DUPLICATE of G3's second cmd
  G8RaptorQ    -> raptorq_proofs

Test files available but NOT invoked by G5/G6/G7:
  e2e_*.rs     (15 additional): e2e_casp, e2e_cluster, e2e_fft, e2e_interpolate,
                e2e_io, e2e_ivp, e2e_ndimage, e2e_optimize, e2e_orchestrator,
                e2e_signal, e2e_sparse, e2e_spatial, e2e_special, e2e_stats
  perf_*.rs    (7 additional): perf_arrayapi, perf_casp, perf_fft, perf_ivp,
                perf_optimize, perf_sparse, perf_special
  diff_*.rs    (2 entirely uncovered): diff_fft, diff_sparse
  evidence_*.rs (11 entirely uncovered): evidence_p2c001..p2c008, p2c012, p2c016

Impact:
1. G3 is MISLABELED. It runs golden_journeys + schema_validation; it does NOT run the differential_<family>_quota_and_structured_logs tests in lib.rs. The gate title 'Differential Conformance' over-promises.
2. G5 E2E gate only covers linalg e2e. Any e2e regression in signal, stats, sparse, fft, special, integrate, cluster, spatial, ndimage, optimize, casp, interpolate, io, ivp is NOT caught by this gate.
3. G6 Performance gate similarly covers only linalg perf.
4. G7 Schema is a duplicate of G3's second command. Running the same test twice wastes budget.
5. G2UnitTests at 'cargo test --workspace' does pick up cargo tests from every crate -- so technically the e2e_* tests ARE invoked through G2, but they run under G2's 300s budget (G5 has 900s). An e2e test that takes >300s to run would fail G2 but silently pass if directly run via G5.

Ask:
1. Split G5 into G5a..G5n per e2e test file; OR invoke 
running 105 tests
test ci_gates::tests::all_gates_have_commands ... ok
test ci_gates::tests::build_gate_result_pass ... ok
test ci_gates::tests::build_gate_result_fail ... ok
test ci_gates::tests::gate_dependencies_form_dag ... ok
test ci_gates::tests::build_pipeline_report_aggregates ... ok
test ci_gates::tests::gate_time_budgets_are_reasonable ... ok
test ci_gates::tests::gate_id_names_are_unique ... ok
test ci_gates::tests::no_circular_dependencies ... ok
test dashboard::tests::dashboard_panel_list_is_stable ... ok
test dashboard::tests::dashboard_navigation_moves_packet_and_case_indices ... ok
test dashboard::tests::dashboard_loader_applies_case_insensitive_packet_filter ... ok
test dashboard::tests::dashboard_loader_skips_malformed_reports ... ok
test forensics::tests::build_replay_cmd_maps_packets ... ok

title: Add E2E conformance tests for stub-remediation: moments, interpolation, BDF Newton
description:
## Context
As stubs are remediated across fsci-stats, fsci-ndimage, and fsci-integrate, we need
E2E conformance tests to verify correctness against SciPy reference values with full
forensic logging per ARTIFACT_TOPOLOGY.md.

## Scope

### 1. E2E stats moments verification (e2e_stats.rs scenarios 11-14)
Add scenarios to crates/fsci-conformance/tests/e2e_stats.rs:

- **e2e_011_closed_form_moments**: Verify Burr12, InvWeibull, TukeyLambda,
  GenHalfLogistic mean/var against SciPy reference values (multiple parameter combos)
- **e2e_012_special_function_moments**: Verify GenLogistic, Gompertz mean/var
  against SciPy reference values
- **e2e_013_piecewise_integral_moments**: Verify CrystalBall, Argus, ExponWeibull
  mean/var against SciPy reference values
- **e2e_014_tukey_lambda_analytical**: Verify TukeyLambda analytical PDF values
  and CDF/PPF roundtrip accuracy

### 2. E2E ndimage interpolation (new test file)
Create crates/fsci-conformance/tests/e2e_ndimage.rs:

- **e2e_001_shift_interpolation**: Compare shift with orders 0,1,3 to SciPy
- **e2e_002_zoom_interpolation**: Compare zoom with orders 0,1,3 to SciPy
- **e2e_003_rotate_interpolation**: Compare rotate with orders 0,1,3 to SciPy
- **e2e_004_map_coordinates**: Compare map_coordinates with orders 0,1,3 to SciPy

### 3. E2E BDF Newton (add to existing integrate tests)
- **e2e_bdf_stiff_convergence**: Van der Pol μ=1000, verify convergence with
  Newton where functional iteration would fail

### Output format
All E2E tests emit topology-compliant artifacts:
- events.jsonl (one JSON line per step)
- summary.json (pass/fail + replay command)
- Under fixtures/artifacts/FSCI-P2C-{NNN}/e2e/runs/{run_id}/{scenario_id}/

## Testing Requirements
- All new scenarios must pass with all_pass = true
- Forensic logs must capture: parameter values, computed moments, reference values,
  absolute error, relative error, tolerance used, pass/fail per step
- Reference values should be hardcoded from SciPy (not computed at test time)

## Dependencies
Depends on: all other beads in this series (moments, interpolation, BDF)

## Validation
```
cargo test -p fsci-conformance --test e2e_stats
cargo test -p fsci-conformance --test e2e_ndimage
cargo test -p fsci-conformance
```
close_reason: All E2E conformance tests already implemented and passing: e2e_stats 011-014 (4 tests), e2e_ndimage (1 test with 4 steps), e2e_ivp BDF Newton (1 test). All pass.

title: Support free variables in linprog and milp
description:
Evidence:
- [crates/fsci-opt/src/lib.rs](/data/projects/frankenscipy/crates/fsci-opt/src/lib.rs:534) rejects `linprog` bounds with `lo = -inf` and explicitly says free variables are not yet supported.
- [crates/fsci-opt/src/lib.rs](/data/projects/frankenscipy/crates/fsci-opt/src/lib.rs:876) applies the same restriction to `milp`.
- SciPy's `linprog`/`milp` surfaces accept unbounded and free variables via `None`/infinite bounds.

Scope:
- Add variable-splitting support for fully free variables and correct handling for one-sided infinite bounds.
- Thread the transformed variables through `linprog` Phase I/II extraction and branch-and-bound node bounds in `milp`.
- Add regression tests for `(-inf, inf)`, `(-inf, hi)`, and mixed bounded/free-variable problems.
- Verify the solution reconstruction and objective value match SciPy semantics.

Suggested files:
- `crates/fsci-opt/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fsci-conformance/tests/e2e_optimize.rs`
close_reason: Already implemented on HEAD

title: [parity-gap] jn_zeros / yn_zeros fail for moderate-to-large order n
description:
scipy.special.jn_zeros(100, 1) returns ~108.836; fsci jn_zeros(100, 1) returns ~120.8 (the McMahon asymptotic guess), missing the true zero by ~12 units. Same defect in yn_zeros.

Root cause: McMahon's asymptotic expansion (DLMF 10.21.19) μ=(4k+2n−1)π/4 is accurate when k>>n, not when n is moderate-to-large. For n=100, k=1, McMahon gives initial ≈ 120.83 but the true first zero of J_100 is ≈ 108.84. The current adaptive bracket-expansion (radius 1, then 8× ±0.3) only reaches ±3.4 from the initial — far too narrow to catch the true zero. The function falls through to 'keep the asymptotic guess' (crates/fsci-special/src/bessel.rs:3160), silently returning a wrong answer.

Repro:
  let zeros = fsci_special::jn_zeros(100, 1);
  let val = fsci_special::jn(100, zeros[0]);  // |val| should be near 0
  // Currently val ≈ J_100(120.83) which is large, NOT a zero.

Existing tests miss this because they only cover n ∈ {0, 1, 2, 3} (crates/fsci-special/src/bessel.rs:4155-4168).

Fix options:
  1) Replace McMahon with Olver/Tricomi expansion (DLMF 10.21.40):
       j_{n,1} ≈ n + 1.85575 n^(1/3) + 1.03315 n^(−1/3) − ...
     valid for the small-k, large-n regime.
  2) Use a piecewise initial guess: McMahon when k > n/2, Olver otherwise.
  3) Widen the adaptive expansion to ±20 with verification that the bracket
     brackets a sign change before bisection.

Acceptance: jn(n, jn_zeros(n, k)[k-1]) is < 1e-6 in absolute value for at
least n ∈ {0, 5, 20, 100} and k ∈ {1, 5, 20}; same for yn_zeros.

title: [b6z3m sub] fsci-special riccati_jn + riccati_yn
description: Slice of frankenscipy-b6z3m. Add scipy.special.riccati_jn(n, x) and scipy.special.riccati_yn(n, x) returning (S_0..=S_n, S'_0..=S'_n) where S_k(x) = x·j_k(x) (riccati_jn) or C_k(x) = -x·y_k(x) (riccati_yn). Closed-form recurrences: S_0 = sin(x), S'_0 = cos(x), then S_{k+1} from spherical Bessel recurrence and S'_k = -k S_k/x + S_{k-1} for k ≥ 1. Add metamorphic relations: S_0(x) = sin(x), C_0(x) = cos(x), recurrence consistency.

title: fuzz(signal): lp2hp robustness harness
description: lp2hp port (2863292) has scipy diff (a08c4a0), unit anchors, and metamorphic involution (098a281) but no libfuzzer harness. Add fuzz_targets/p2c009_signal_lp2hp_robustness.rs paralleling the lp2lp harness in b67766d. Same Arbitrary-derived (b, a, wo) inputs, same length-preservation contract, same no-NaN invariant (allowing ±∞ where scipy.signal.lp2hp matches via normalize_filter denormal-overflow). Add 5 seed corpora.
close_reason: Shipped fuzz/fuzz_targets/p2c009_signal_lp2hp_robustness.rs paralleling the lp2lp harness in b67766d. Drives Arbitrary-derived (b, a, wo) sanitized to finite + wo > 0, asserts: (1) never panics in success path; (2) output b length = max(nb_in, na_in) — lp2hp's s→wo/s substitution pads the numerator to the denominator's degree before normalization; (3) output a length in [1, max(nb_in, na_in)] (normalize_filter may trim leading zeros); (4) na[0] = 1.0; (5) no NaN (±∞ allowed for scipy parity in denormal-tiny corner). 5 seed corpora paralleling lp2lp. 200,000 runs clean. Added [[bin]] entry to fuzz/Cargo.toml.

title: [signal] lp2bs_zpk lowpass-to-bandstop ZPK transform
description: scipy.signal.lp2bs_zpk. Equivalent to compose lp2hp then bandpass-style split: zhp = bw/(2z), pair-split via sqrt(zhp² - wo²), append degree zeros at ±i·wo, gain k * Re(prod(-z)/prod(-p)).

title: fuzz: pdist + is_valid_dm/is_valid_y validators have no fuzz coverage
description: Skill: /testing-fuzzing. The is_valid_dm, is_valid_y, num_obs_dm, num_obs_y helpers I just shipped (commit 33c22f3) lack a fuzz harness. p2c011_spatial_squareform.rs only covers the squareform_to_matrix↔squareform_to_condensed roundtrip on raw f64 input, not the validator+pdist composition. A new harness p2c011_spatial_pdist_validators.rs will: (1) generate random X (rows×dim), (2) compute pdist(X) for each metric, (3) verify is_valid_y(pdist) and num_obs_y(pdist) == X.len(), (4) verify squareform(pdist) is is_valid_dm, (5) verify the matrix has zero diagonal and is symmetric. Catches any future regression in the validators or pdist's invariants.
close_reason: Shipped fuzz/fuzz_targets/p2c011_spatial_pdist_validators.rs (registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml) with 5-seed corpus. Compiles via cargo check --bin p2c011_spatial_pdist_validators. Exercises pdist across 8 metric variants and verifies five invariants: pdist length = n(n-1)/2, non-negative entries, is_valid_y acceptance, num_obs_y inverse, and full squareform↔condensed roundtrip with is_valid_dm at 1e-9 tolerance.

title: fsci-stats: Fisk.pdf and Mielke.pdf wrong at x=0 boundary
description: Fisk.pdf and Mielke.pdf in crates/fsci-stats/src/lib.rs short-circuit to 0 for x <= 0, but at x=0 the formulas have a shape-dependent limit. Fisk.pdf = c·x^(c-1)/(1+x^c)² → ∞ for c<1, → 1 for c==1, → 0 for c>1 (scipy returns inf/1/0 in those regimes). Mielke.pdf = k·x^(k-1)/(1+x^s)^(1+k/s) → ∞ for k<1, → 1 for k==1, → 0 for k>1. Continuation of the BetaDist (9e4bfd9) and Arcsine (39431bb) boundary fix rotation. Add boundary regression tests for both distributions covering all three regimes.
close_reason: [LOW] Fixed Fisk.pdf and Mielke.pdf boundary defects at x=0 in crates/fsci-stats/src/lib.rs (continuation of BetaDist 9e4bfd9 + Arcsine 39431bb rotation). Both distributions short-circuited to 0 for x<=0, but at exactly x=0 the formulas have shape-dependent limits per scipy. Fisk.pdf(0) = c·0^(c-1)/(1+0)² → +∞ for c<1, → 1 for c=1, → 0 for c>1. Mielke.pdf(0) follows the same pattern in k. New code branches on shape vs 1 at x=0. 2 regression tests pin all three regimes plus outside-support behavior. fsci-stats --lib: 1040 → 1042.

title: [io] reject non-32-bit IEEE float WAV reads
description:
## What

wav_read currently treats IEEE float (audio_format=3) with 8/16/24-bit sample sizes as PCM, which silently mis-decodes malformed WAV headers.

## Fix

- Reject audio_format=3 unless bits_per_sample == 32 (the only float width we implement).
- Add a regression test for a float-format header with 16-bit samples.

## Why

Avoids mis-decoding malformed files and makes behavior explicit for unsupported float widths.
close_reason: Rejected IEEE float WAV with non-32-bit samples and added regression test
